reasoning: I observe a direct relationship between voltage, current, and resistance based on Ohm's law (V = IR). I will now increase the current to see how it affects the voltage.
I have observed that increasing the current increases the voltage, while keeping the resistance constant. Now I will decrease the current to see if the relationship holds true.
I have observed that increasing the current increases the voltage and decreasing the current decreases the voltage. Now I will increase the resistance to see if it impacts voltage.
I have observed that voltage is directly proportional to current and inversely proportional to resistance. I will now decrease the resistance to see if it affects the voltage.
I have established a clear relationship between voltage, current, and resistance. Voltage is directly proportional to the product of current and resistance. Therefore, V = I * R.
